commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ba4f1433f809fad1afc57176db693bc8d6a4d6d6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `model: "inherit"` support for native `sessions_spawn`, propagates requester model context into subagent spawning, preserves alias precedence, and updates docs, tests, and prompt snapshots.

PR surface: Source +208, Tests +298, Docs +10. Total +516 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR adding an intentional `model: "inherit"` mode, not a bug report with a current-main failure path. The supplied proof and tests demonstrate the new behavior at PR head.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is indicated; the remaining action is normal maintainer review plus current-head CI/changed-gate confirmation.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads the requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Preserves compatibility for an existing model alias named `inherit`: aliases resolve before the inheritance sentinel is applied.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior, alias precedence, and config precedence.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, alias compatibility around the `inherit` sentinel, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session, while preserving any configured model alias named `"inherit"`.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `ba4f1433f8`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`; local loopback Gateway started with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; real provider-backed run on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` using an OAuth auth profile.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  docs/gateway/config-agents.md \
  docs/tools/subagents.md \
  src/agents/model-selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-selection.ts \
  src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts

pnpm test \
  src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts

pnpm check:changed

OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs

node scripts/run-node.mjs agent \
  --agent main \
  --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-retry-002 \
  --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 \
  --timeout 900 \
  --json \
  --message 'PR verification run for head ba4f1433f8. Do not send any external channel message and do not use the message tool. Use sessions_spawn exactly once with taskName="inherit_model_probe_after_alias_fix", label="inherit-model-probe-after-alias-fix", model="inherit", context="isolated", cleanup="keep", runTimeoutSeconds=180, and task="Return exactly CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_ALIAS_FIX_PROBE_OK, then include one short line naming the model/provider you are running under if visible." After spawning, call sessions_yield so the child completion can return to this requester session. When the child result arrives, reply with a concise proof summary including the child taskName, the child result text, and the requester model openai-codex/gpt-5.5.'

node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --all-agents --active 120 --json
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture excerpts, redacted to omit local absolute paths):

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 8.17s
```

```text
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
```

```text
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests, docs, tooling
...
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

```json
{
  "runId": "021eddb9-9d7c-4f02-8cfd-1ba42144137f",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "result": {
    "meta": {
      "agentMeta": {
        "provider": "openai-codex",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "agentHarnessId": "codex"
      },
      "executionTrace": {
        "winnerProvider": "openai-codex",
        "winnerModel": "gpt-5.5",
        "fallbackUsed": false
      },
      "toolSummary": {
        "calls": 2,
        "tools": ["sessions_spawn", "sessions_yield"],
        "failures": 0
      }
    }
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "parentToolCall": {
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "taskName": "inherit_model_probe_after_alias_fix",
      "label": "inherit-model-probe-after-alias-fix",
      "model": "inherit",
      "context": "isolated",
      "cleanup": "keep",
      "runTimeoutSeconds": 180
    }
  },
  "spawnResult": {
    "status": "accepted",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:80499f9c-27b6-439a-a4cd-fa95ea2ee570",
    "taskName": "inherit_model_probe_after_alias_fix",
    "modelApplied": true
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "key": "agent:main:subagent:80499f9c-27b6-439a-a4cd-fa95ea2ee570",
  "kind": "spawn-child",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "modelProvider": "openai-codex",
  "providerOverride": "openai-codex",
  "modelOverride": "gpt-5.5",
  "agentRuntime": {
    "id": "codex",
    "source": "implicit"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "childAssistantMessage": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "model": "gpt-5.5",
    "content": "CHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_ALIAS_FIX_PROBE_OK\nModel/provider visible: GPT-5 via Codex/OpenAI."
  },
  "parentFinalMessage": "Proof summary:\n\nTask: `inherit_model_probe_after_alias_fix`\n\nChild result:\n```text\nCHILD_MODEL_INHERIT_ALIAS_FIX_PROBE_OK\nModel/provider visible: GPT-5 via Codex/OpenAI.\n```\n\nRequester model: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the parent session ran on `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, invoked `sessions_spawn` with `model: "inherit"`, the spawn was accepted with `modelApplied: true`, and the child `spawn-child` session persisted and executed with `modelProvider: "openai-codex"` and `model: "gpt-5.5"`. Focused regression tests also verify that a configured alias named `"inherit"` remains a concrete model selection instead of triggering inheritance.
- What was not tested: external channel delivery was intentionally disabled with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; ACP runtime inheritance remains out of scope for this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof used a local loopback Gateway and a real model provider run, with channel connectors skipped to avoid external delivery.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path or alias-compatibility coverage for an alias named `"inherit"`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-retry-002 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --all-agents --active 120 --json`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, missing requester model behavior, and a model alias named `"inherit"` winning over the sentinel.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model. The first implementation also treated bare `"inherit"` as a sentinel before alias resolution; this follow-up preserves existing aliases named `"inherit"`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value when no model alias named `"inherit"` exists.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model and alias precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only after alias resolution, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, direct-spawn, and live Gateway seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper to re-run on `ba4f1433f8`, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The PR body now includes live Gateway proof and alias-compatibility validation for the latest PR head. Waiting on automated checks and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's live Gateway proof request and the compatibility finding about a model alias named `"inherit"`.
target.author: gausons

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
